name: Mammary Tumours
description: Mammary tumours are among the most common tumours in intact female dogs, with approximately 50% being malignant. Early spaying significantly reduces risk. Malignant forms can metastasise to the lungs.
treatment_overview: Surgical excision (lumpectomy, simple mastectomy, or regional chain mastectomy) is the primary treatment. Chemotherapy (doxorubicin) for high-grade malignant cases. Concurrent ovariohysterectomy may reduce recurrence.
prevention_tips: Spaying before first or second season dramatically reduces lifetime risk
Regular mammary palpation in intact females from middle age
affected_breeds: all intact female breeds (spaniels, poodles, dachshunds at higher relative risk)
